article_title: Oracle Cuts Up to 30,000 Jobs in Largest AI-Driven Restructuring of 2026, Redirecting Billions Toward Data Center Buildout

content_preview.title: Oracle Cuts Up to 30,000 Jobs in Largest AI-Driven Restructuring of 2026, Redirecting Billions Toward Data Center Buildout
content_preview.summary: Oracle terminates an estimated 18 percent of its global workforce via early-morning emails with no prior warning, freeing up to $10 billion in cash flow to finance a $156 billion AI infrastructure expansion even as the company reports a 95 percent jump in quarterly net income.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Oracle began executing one of the largest workforce reductions in the technology sector's history on March 31, 2026, cutting an estimated 20,000 to 30,000 employees — roughly 18 percent of its 162,000-person global workforce — to redirect capital toward artificial intelligence infrastructure, according to [The Next Web](https://thenextweb.com/news/oracle-layoffs-march-2026). The company has not publicly confirmed the total number of affected positions, but investment bank TD Cowen e...

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All financial figures, workforce numbers, and timeline verified.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral. Covers both the scale of cuts and financial rationale.
